mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months indicates rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in Industrial Production (INDPRO) over 6 months indicates rising supply and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and a 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over 3 months signal a tightening monetary cycle and labor market, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) and a 0.5% increase in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over 3 months signal a weakening demand and labor market, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) and a 0.5% decrease in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) over 3 months signal a weakening labor market and demand, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% decrease in Industrial Production (INDPRO) and a 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over 6 months signal a weakening supply and tightening monetary cycle, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite negative signal.
